name: Crest
why: Consistently pushed technical boundaries for decades. Masters of everything from coding to music to design.

name: Booze Design
why: Artistic vision combined with jaw-dropping code. Their demos feel like complete audiovisual experiences.

name: Oxyron
why: Elite coders known for impossible visual effects. Graham's coding is legendary.

name: Fairlight
why: One of the oldest and most respected groups, spanning cracking and demos across platforms.

name: Censor Design
why: Major Swedish group with enormous output and multiple party wins. Technical excellence across decades.

name: Performers
why: Technical wizards, especially in the 2010s+ era with effects previously thought impossible.

name: Bonzai
why: Prolific and creative, strong in both demos and intros with distinctive style.

name: Reflex
why: Active since 1993, strong demos and music.

name: Genesis Project
why: Long-running Swedish powerhouse, consistent quality across decades.
mustSee: Memento Mori (2020), X Marks the Spot (2018)

name: Resource
why: Known for polished, well-designed productions with strong all-round skills.

name: Triad
why: Swedish legends — enormous output, cracking and demos, one of the most enduring groups.

name: Offence
why: Strong modern-era group with impressive, technically advanced releases.
mustSee: We Are Demo (w/ Fairlight & Noice, 2016), Lifecycle (w/ Prosonix, 2022)

name: Desire
why: Hungarian group with decades of consistent party placements and polished productions.

name: Horizon
why: Swedish golden-era powerhouse. Some of the fastest 3D vector routines ever on C64.
mustSee: The Last Traktor III (1992), Amplifire (w/ Instinct, 2008)

name: Onslaught
why: Strong modern-era group with critically acclaimed, technically impressive demos.
